steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers found that bulkheads are more strongly negatively correlated with waterbird community integrity than riprap. To illustrate this finding, you need a choice that directly compares a high-bulkhead site to a high-riprap site and shows the bulkhead site scoring lower on the IWCI.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Curtis (high bulkheads + riprap combined) vs. Onancock (low bulkheads + riprap combined)': Compares a mixed-hardening site to a low-hardening site. It never isolates bulkheads versus riprap, so it cannot show that bulkheads are worse than riprap. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Stony vs. Old Road, both high-bulkhead sites, statistically insignificant difference': Both sites have more bulkheads than riprap, so there is no bulkhead-versus-riprap contrast at all. A comparison within the same type proves nothing about which type is more harmful. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Old Road (high bulkhead) lower IWCI than Miles (high riprap)': Old Road is a high-bulkhead site and Miles is a high-riprap site. Old Road scoring lower directly shows that the bulkhead-heavy site has worse waterbird community integrity than the riprap-heavy site, exactly illustrating the researchers’ finding. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Curtis (equal bulkheads and riprap) higher IWCI than Miles (unequal percentages)': Comparing equal-percentage hardening to unequal-percentage hardening does not test bulkhead versus riprap. The outcome could be explained by total hardening amount, not type. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
